source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/ether-etf-outflow-day-inflow-streak-billions-eth-price-predictions?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Spot Ether ETFs Experience Outflows After Significant Inflows
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, spot Ether exchange-traded funds (ETFs) concluded the week with notable outflows, ending a streak of substantial inflows that lasted nearly two weeks. On Friday, U.S.-spot Ether (ETH) ETFs recorded $59.3 million in outflows, marking the conclusion of an eight-day inflow period that had accumulated approximately $3.7 billion, as reported by Farside data.

This development coincides with Ether's recent price movements, where it fell just 1.94% short of reaching its 2021 all-time high of $4,878 on Thursday, before retreating to $4,448 at the time of publication, according to CoinMarketCap. The cessation of the inflow streak serves as a significant indicator for Ether traders, who are likely to monitor ETF flows closely in the coming weeks. These flows, along with potential developments in Ether treasury firm acquisitions, will play a crucial role in determining whether Ether can reclaim its 2021 all-time highs.

Since their inception in July 2024, spot Ether ETFs have experienced $12.68 billion in total net flows, as noted by Farside. Analysts emphasize the importance of sustained ETF inflows for Ether to achieve its all-time highs once again. Nansen analyst Jake Kennis remarked that the rally would persist as long as the flows and narrative remain robust. Crypto trader Langerius suggested that Ether could reach a price of $10,000 if consistent weekly inflows continue. Meanwhile, crypto trader Merlijin The Trader observed on Thursday that ETF inflows have surged dramatically, describing it as institutional FOMO.

Ether has seen a 29.63% increase over the past 30 days, according to CoinMarketCap. However, sentiment platform Santiment noted that crypto traders are not expressing as much bullishness for Ether on social media compared to Bitcoin. Historically, this could indicate that Ether may slightly outperform Bitcoin in the short term. Cointelegraph also reported that Ethereum's staking ecosystem has reached new heights, with 877,106 Ether valued at $3.88 billion currently queued for withdrawal.

While this increase in queued unstaked ETH might suggest significant profit-taking, DeFi analyst Ignas pointed out that the recent accumulation and buying strength from Ether treasury companies and spot Ether ETFs are mitigating much of the selling pressure. This dynamic highlights the ongoing interest and investment in Ether, despite fluctuations in ETF flows and market sentiment.

data.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum Sentiment Analysis Reveals Market Trends
data.description: According to PANews, Santiment recently tweeted that Bitcoin's peak greed levels have coincided perfectly with its all-time highs and local peaks. Meanwhile, despite Ethereum's significant outperformance against Bitcoin over the past three months, its bullish sentiment remains considerably lower. Typically, price movements tend to contradict retail investors' expectations. As a result, due to the market's relatively low interest in buying Ethereum during dips compared to Bitcoin, the current outlook suggests that Ethereum's bullish trend is slightly higher than that of Bitcoin.

data.title: Ethereum's Growth Driven by Institutional Investment and Regulatory Changes
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Ethereum's recent price surge is primarily fueled by institutional purchases, with significant contributions from U.S.-based entities. Additionally, Asia is increasingly embracing Ethereum, with Japan and South Korea emerging as key markets in the region. These countries may experience a substantial influx of institutional capital following the implementation of new regulations.
